title: schubertia disticha var. pendula
intro: Schubertia disticha var. pendula, also known as the 'Hanging Schubertia', is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the Apocynaceae family. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America and is known for its unique, hanging habit and beautiful purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Schubertia disticha var. pendula include infusions and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
